library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream
identity.titleArabic:
Convenience function for straightforward audio init for the common
case

summary.actualTranslationArabic:
Convenience function for straightforward audio init for the common
case
summary.actualMeaningArabic: تمثل هذه الدالة عملية تشغيل SDL Open الصوت الجهاز الدفق داخل SDL3، وتنفذ فعلاً عند استدعائها لا مجرد تعريف نظري.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى تشغيل SDL Open الصوت الجهاز الدفق داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic:
القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه ( SDL_AudioStream *) Returns an
audio stream on success, ready to use, or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information. When
done with this stream, call SDL_DestroyAudioStream to free
resources and close the device. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription:
Convenience function for straightforward audio init for the common
case.

signature.raw: SDL_AudioStream * SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream(SDL_AudioDeviceID devid, const SDL_AudioSpec *spec, SDL_AudioStreamCallback callback, void *userdata);

parameters.name: devid
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل devid من النوع SDL_AudioDeviceID القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
an audio device to open, or SDL_AUDIO_DEVICE_DEFAULT_PLAYBACK
or SDL_AUDIO_DEVICE_DEFAULT_RECORDING .

parameters.name: spec
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل spec من النوع const SDL_AudioSpec * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: the audio stream's data format. Can be NULL.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل callback من النوع SDL_AudioStreamCallback القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
a callback where the app will provide new data for playback, or
receive new data for recording. Can be NULL, in which case the app will
need to call SDL_PutAudioStreamData or SDL_GetAudioStreamData as
necessary.

parameters.name: userdata
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل userdata من النوع void * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription:
app-controlled pointer passed to callback. Can be NULL. Ignored if
callback is NULL.

remarks:
If all your app intends to do is provide a single source of PCM
audio, this function allows you to do all your audio setup in a single
call.

This is also intended to be a clean means to migrate apps from
SDL2.

This function will open an audio device, create a stream and bind it.
Unlike other methods of setup, the audio device will be closed when this
stream is destroyed, so the app can treat the returned SDL_AudioStream as the only object
needed to manage audio playback.

Also unlike other functions, the audio device begins paused. This is
to map more closely to SDL2-style behavior, since there is no extra step
here to bind a stream to begin audio flowing. The audio device should be
resumed with SDL_ResumeAudioStreamDevice ().
This function works with both playback and recording devices.

The spec parameter represents the app's side of the
audio stream. That is, for recording audio, this will be the output
format, and for playing audio, this will be the input format. If spec is
NULL, the system will choose the format, and the app can use SDL_GetAudioStreamFormat () to
obtain this information later.

If you don't care about opening a specific audio device, you can (and
probably should ), use SDL_AUDIO_DEVICE_DEFAULT_PLAYBACK
for playback and SDL_AUDIO_DEVICE_DEFAULT_RECORDING
for recording.

One can optionally provide a callback function; if NULL, the app is
expected to queue audio data for playback (or unqueue audio data if
capturing). Otherwise, the callback will begin to fire once the device
is unpaused.

Destroying the returned stream with SDL_DestroyAudioStream will also
close the audio device associated with this stream.
details.returns:
( SDL_AudioStream *) Returns an
audio stream on success, ready to use, or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information. When
done with this stream, call SDL_DestroyAudioStream to free
resources and close the device.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
